title: Accounting and Finance for Construction
catalogDescription: Introduction to construction oriented financial management. Explains why construction financial management is different than financial management in other industries. Describes how to account for a company's financial resources and how to build a company's accounting system. Prerequisite: graduate standing. Offered: W.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: graduate standing.
prerequisite text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/minimum-grade semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Energy Infrastructure and the Environment
catalogDescription: Focuses on energy infrastructure, including site selection, permitting, design, construction, and maintenance. Includes electrical production facilities as well as transmission, focusing on permitting and construction of renewable energy facilities. Covers renewable energy infrastructure, emphasizing wind, solar, and geothermal. Offered: A.

title: Statistical Fundamentals for Construction and Materials Applications
catalogDescription: Overview of statistical measures used in various construction and materials decision-making processes. Subjects include data distributions, hypothesis tests (making decisions with statistics), regression analysis, sampling, quality control and assurance, and experimental design. Uses construction data to illustrate these measures. Offered: Sp.

title: Computer-Aided Construction
catalogDescription: Application of information technology to construction management and cost estimating. Topics include, but not limited to, computerized construction, fundamentals of computer hardware, construction management software tools, web publishing, GPS application, and construction data management. Offered: S.

title: Construction Materials
catalogDescription: Examines the use of aggregates, bituminous mixtures, Portland cement concrete, roller compacted concrete, soil and site stabilization, utility cuts, and flowable backfill in construction projects. Emphasis on behavior of materials in various construction applications. Offered: W.

title: Pavement Construction
catalogDescription: Examines pavement construction, including pavement contracts and specifications, quality control and assurance programs, and plant and laydown operations. Reviews both national and international pavement construction practices. Offered: Sp.
